jokes.text: Well, where's the law that says the B has to stand for bacon?
jokes.setup: Customers complaining about no bacon in BLTs
jokes.punchline: Carlton's pedantic logic that B doesn't have to mean bacon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - overthinking simple things with misplaced confidence. The pedantic wordplay fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Bread, lettuce and tomato?
jokes.setup: Carlton's claim that B doesn't have to stand for bacon
jokes.punchline: Will's incredulous interpretation of what BLT would mean without bacon
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response that builds on Carlton's absurdity, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Well, we had to cut back somewhere. Last month, our outlay exceeded our revenues. But I don't expect you to understand that.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to justify removing bacon
jokes.punchline: His condescending business speak about a simple sandwich shop
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton pompousness - using business jargon for basic decisions and being condescending to Will.

jokes.text: [MIMICS CARLTON] But I don't expect you to understand that.
jokes.setup: Carlton just condescended to Will
jokes.punchline: Will immediately mocks Carlton's condescending tone
jokes.explanation: Quick callback with good mimicry - Will's signature move of immediately deflating Carlton's pretensions.

jokes.text: Oh, that's easy. - Will Smith.
jokes.setup: Denise asks for 9-letter word meaning 'incredible'
jokes.punchline: Will answers with his own real name
jokes.explanation: Meta joke that breaks fourth wall - Will Smith the actor answering as Will the character. Economical and character-appropriate cockiness.

jokes.text: [MIMICS BOGART] Of all the campus dames in all the campuses in all the world... why'd you have to walk into mine?
jokes.setup: Will trying to be smooth with movie reference
jokes.punchline: Adapting famous Casablanca line to college setting
jokes.explanation: Decent adaptation of classic line to college setting, shows Will's charm and pop culture knowledge.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, dude is cool. He ain't no Shaft, though, you know?
jokes.setup: Denise praising Humphrey Bogart
jokes.punchline: Will's street perspective that Shaft is cooler than Bogart
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Will's street sensibility preferring blaxploitation hero to classic Hollywood icon.

jokes.text: You know, most women can't appreciate the subtle nuances of a fine Shaft film.
jokes.setup: Denise appreciating Shaft reference
jokes.punchline: Will's pretentious description of Shaft films as having 'subtle nuances'
jokes.explanation: Ironic contrast between lowbrow Shaft films and highbrow critical language - classic Will mixing street and pretension.

jokes.text: WILL [THINKING]: You ain't wearing no bra.
jokes.setup: Denise asks what Will is thinking
jokes.punchline: His actual thoughts revealed to audience
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom male gaze joke - economical but relies on cheap sexual humor rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: So do you like the Pointer Sisters?
jokes.setup: Will caught thinking inappropriate thoughts
jokes.punchline: His awkward subject change to music
jokes.explanation: Awkward transition joke that's functional but predictable - the random topic change after being caught.

jokes.text: Well, that explains the attraction.
jokes.setup: Both discovering they're from Philly
jokes.punchline: Denise suggesting their connection is more than coincidence
jokes.explanation: Smooth flirtation line that builds on their shared background - economical but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Potatoes. - Oh, girl. Look at you, you are just like me. Except, you know, a little softer and you smell better.
jokes.setup: Will testing their compatibility with rapid-fire questions
jokes.punchline: His conclusion that she's like him but with feminine qualities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's charm and the humor in finding his female equivalent.

jokes.text: What's a six-letter word that comes at the end of the rhyme: Miss me, miss me Now you gotta... - Kiss me. - I'd love to.
jokes.setup: Denise setting up childhood rhyme as crossword clue
jokes.punchline: Will's answer leading to her suggestive response
jokes.explanation: Clever setup using childhood rhyme as seduction technique - well-structured with good misdirection.

jokes.text: [SCREAMS]
jokes.setup: Romantic moment building between Will and Denise
jokes.punchline: Will's panicked scream as he realizes the situation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that punctures romantic tension - economical and effective character moment.

jokes.text: Why are you asking me? I ain't never been never attracted to nobody in my whole life.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking Will about attraction after Will just met Denise
jokes.punchline: Will's obvious lie about never being attracted to anyone
jokes.explanation: Transparent lie that audience knows is false - good character moment showing Will's guilt.

jokes.text: I wasn't thinking about his butt. - Hey, I am now.
jokes.setup: Ashley denying she was thinking about someone's butt
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting Will put the idea in her head
jokes.explanation: Good quick reversal - the power of suggestion humor executed cleanly.

jokes.text: But every once in a while... it's kind of like the Civil War. You know, the North got everything under control... but every once in a while, it's a Southern uprising.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his attraction to other women despite loving Lisa
jokes.punchline: Extended Civil War metaphor for internal conflict
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor comparing sexual temptation to Civil War - original and quotable concept.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, look, I'm trying, man... but I got the feeling that the South shall rise again.
jokes.setup: Continuing Civil War metaphor
jokes.punchline: Famous Confederate slogan applied to sexual temptation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that extends the metaphor with famous historical phrase - highly quotable and clever.

jokes.text: Oh, wait, don't get me wrong, Uncle Phil, once I say I do, I don't.
jokes.setup: Will assuring Phil about his commitment to marriage
jokes.punchline: Playing with wedding vows language 'I do/I don't'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with wedding vow language - economical and clever structure that flips meaning perfectly.

jokes.text: [IN HIGH-PITCHED VOICE] Oh, come on, pudding, jeez... you pushing me all off the sidewalk. - Oh, for God's sake, Will. Would you please be my wife?
jokes.setup: Phil asking Will to role-play as Vivian
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated feminine voice and Phil's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy bit with Will's exaggerated feminine impression - character-appropriate but fairly standard.

jokes.text: Would you please be my wife?
jokes.setup: Will's terrible attempt at playing Vivian
jokes.punchline: Phil's exasperated, direct request
jokes.explanation: Phil's deadpan frustration with Will's performance. Good character moment.

jokes.text: A mean left hook.
jokes.setup: Phil asking what Vivian has that other women don't
jokes.punchline: Phil's deadpan reference to Vivian's violent tendencies
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery about domestic violence played for laughs - economical and character-appropriate for Phil.

jokes.text: I don't care if Toni girl goes galloping by on a white horse buck-naked, you know?
jokes.setup: Will claiming he has temptation under control
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and absurd scenario with Toni Braxton
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific absurd scenario - the white horse detail makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: She better be traveling fast, though. Snatch her little butt down off there. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Will just claimed he wouldn't be tempted by naked Toni Braxton on horseback
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with what he'd actually do
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction that reveals Will's true nature - great structure undermining his own claim.

jokes.text: Uh... It's a rare condition. Day blindness. You know, they're really sensitive.
jokes.setup: Will wearing sunglasses indoors when Denise arrives
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous made-up medical condition to explain sunglasses
jokes.explanation: Absurd excuse that audience knows is false - 'day blindness' is a funny made-up condition.

jokes.text: And then there's your face.
jokes.setup: Will complimenting Denise's dimples and cheeks
jokes.punchline: Awkward addition that suggests he was talking about her butt cheeks
jokes.explanation: Double entendre about 'cheeks' - economical but relies on sexual innuendo rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I love her. Of course I do. I have to. We're engaged.
jokes.setup: Denise asking if Will really loves Lisa
jokes.punchline: Will's increasingly unconvincing reasons for loving Lisa
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's uncertainty - the obligation-based love is sadly funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, I know, only too well.
jokes.setup: Will saying he'd be 'all over' Denise if this was a year ago
jokes.punchline: Denise's knowing response suggesting Will's reputation
jokes.explanation: Subtle implication about Will's past behavior - economical but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: To myself. Yeah. You know, I do that sometimes. Don't you? Yeah, yeah, you do. You do.
jokes.setup: Lisa catching Will talking to someone
jokes.punchline: Will's increasingly panicked explanation that he talks to himself
jokes.explanation: Standard panicked cover-up scene - functional but predictable cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, I've thought about that, but it probably won't help.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning a shower
jokes.punchline: Will thinking she means he should take a shower (presumably cold)
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection on 'shower' meaning - Will's guilt makes him think she knows about his attraction.

jokes.text: Excuse me, but I couldn't help overhearing from where I was eavesdropping...
jokes.setup: Carlton joining the conversation
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of intentional eavesdropping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton line - pretentious phrasing around admitting bad behavior. Very quotable.

jokes.text: People are talking about you too, but that don't mean they want you at their party.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming people are talking about their food
jokes.punchline: Will's burn about people talking about Carlton negatively
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that deflates Carlton's boasting - well-structured insult with good economy.

jokes.text: Hey. Hey, hey, hold up a second there, Chef-Boy-Are-You-Short.
jokes.setup: Carlton acting like a chef
jokes.punchline: Will's pun on Chef Boyardee combined with height joke
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that combines brand name with Carlton's height - character-specific and memorable.

jokes.text: [MIMICS BOO-BOO] 'Yogi... I don't think the ranger's gonna like that.'
jokes.setup: Kevin showing Ashley his Boo Boo impression
jokes.punchline: Adult doing cartoon character voice
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Kevin's immaturity - functional but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Hey, you're not exactly splitting atoms either, okay?
jokes.setup: Kevin's simple response 'I don't know'
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic comment about Kevin's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic putdown using atomic physics metaphor - economical and fits Will's character.

jokes.text: Oh, uh, it was an unexpected oral exam. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about Will's 'test' with temptation
jokes.punchline: Will's double entendre about oral exam
jokes.explanation: Sexual double entendre that's predictable - relies on innuendo rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Miss me, miss me Now you gotta...
jokes.setup: Carlton recognizing Denise from earlier crossword encounter
jokes.punchline: Carlton attempting to recreate the flirtatious moment
jokes.explanation: Callback showing Carlton's awkward attempt at Will's smooth moves - good character moment.

jokes.text: Gorgeous, no. Man, I'm not. I got big ears and I got a big high butt, you know?
jokes.setup: Denise calling Will gorgeous in front of Lisa
jokes.punchline: Will's panicked self-deprecation with specific body parts
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor to deflect attention - the specific 'high butt' detail makes it character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, Will, I don't bite. Unless, of course, you want me to.
jokes.setup: Will being nervous around Denise
jokes.punchline: Denise's suggestive comment about biting
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo - functional but predictable and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Girl, you're like a black Heather Locklear, ain't you?
jokes.setup: Denise being seductive and manipulative
jokes.punchline: Will comparing her to soap opera villain Heather Locklear
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that captures Denise's manipulative nature - economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Nothing, you know, just suddenly got a little hot in here. Brother looking for a little shade.
jokes.setup: Will hiding behind something when asked to give Denise a ride
jokes.punchline: Will's excuse about getting hot and needing shade
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of Will literally hiding - the shade metaphor works with his avoidance.

jokes.text: Baby, where's Nicky's skateboard?
jokes.setup: Denise needing a ride home 11 miles
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting a skateboard for an 11-mile journey
jokes.explanation: Absurd solution to avoid being alone with Denise - the specificity of the skateboard makes it funny.

jokes.text: I'll do you. It! It! I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Will reluctantly agreeing to give Denise a ride
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip revealing his true desires
jokes.explanation: Classic Freudian slip - economical and reveals character's internal conflict, though predictable.

jokes.text: Hey. Hey, look, we are only four floors up. I ain't got no problem doing a Wesley Snipes out this window.
jokes.setup: Will trapped in car with Denise
jokes.punchline: Reference to Wesley Snipes action movies about jumping out window
jokes.explanation: Good action movie reference showing Will's desperation - using actor's name as verb is clever.

jokes.text: Bad Denise. Bad Denise.
jokes.setup: Denise being seductive
jokes.punchline: Will treating her like a misbehaving pet
jokes.explanation: Treating person like dog is absurd and economical - shows Will's attempt to maintain control.

jokes.text: Well, only if I missed and hit the wall.
jokes.setup: Denise asking if he'd cry if someone rejected him
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation about physically throwing himself
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation joke - plays with the phrase 'throw yourself at someone' in unexpected way.

jokes.text: Oh. I don't believe I fell for that. - I invented that.
jokes.setup: Will falling for classic seduction technique
jokes.punchline: His claim that he invented the move
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's ego even when caught - claims credit for falling for seduction.

jokes.text: What is that, satin? - Silk.
jokes.setup: Will trying to resist temptation
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by fabric quality
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's weakness through mundane detail - his resistance crumbling over fabric texture.

jokes.text: I love Lucy. - That ain't it, is it? - It's Lisa.
jokes.setup: Will trying to remember his fiancée's name under pressure
jokes.punchline: Mixing up Lisa with Lucy Ricardo
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom reference under pressure - forgetting fiancée's name for famous TV character is relatable panic.

jokes.text: Keep the car.
jokes.setup: Will rejecting Denise and walking away
jokes.punchline: Denise's frustrated response to throw keys at him
jokes.explanation: Economical expression of Denise's frustration - the car keys become dramatic gesture.

jokes.text: Wow. You know, that's deep, G. - Hey, where'd you get that? - Popeye.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey giving profound advice about honesty being your North Star
jokes.punchline: Revealing he got the wisdom from Popeye cartoons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - profound philosophical advice deflated by cartoon source. Perfect structure and unexpected revelation.

jokes.text: Like, I'm gonna listen to Geoffrey. When's the last time you seen him with a woman?
jokes.setup: Will deciding whether to take Geoffrey's advice about honesty
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Geoffrey's relationship wisdom due to his single status
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's flawed logic - dismissing good advice based on advisor's personal life.
